problem_text: Classify sum_{n=1}^infinity (-1)^n/sqrt(n) as absolutely convergent, conditionally convergent, or divergent.

content.label: Alternating series form
content.applies_when: the terms alternate in sign: a_n = (-1)^n b_n with b_n = 1/sqrt(n) > 0.

content.meaning: if b_n > 0, b_n is decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, then the alternating series sum (-1)^n b_n converges.

content.label: check b_n = 1/sqrt(n) is positive, decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, so the series converges
content.action: check b_n = 1/sqrt(n) is positive, decreasing, and lim b_n = 0, so the series converges
content.purpose: establish convergence of the alternating series

content.meaning: sum 1/n^p converges iff p > 1; sum 1/n^(1/2) has p = 1/2 < 1 and diverges.

content.label: test sum 1/sqrt(n) = sum 1/n^(1/2), a p-series with p = 1/2 < 1, which diverges
content.action: test sum 1/sqrt(n) = sum 1/n^(1/2), a p-series with p = 1/2 < 1, which diverges
content.purpose: decide whether the convergence is absolute

content.label: Conditional convergence
content.applies_when: the series converges but the series of absolute values diverges.

content.label: the series converges but not absolutely, so it is conditionally convergent
content.action: the series converges but not absolutely, so it is conditionally convergent
